items.summary: Behind the scenes of the Guts tour: a roaming, solar-powered tour that snakes its way through some of the most remote communities in the Northern Territory, bringing together bands from the city and the bush to hold concerts in areas that don't host a lot of touring musicians.

items.title: Australia's doubles fly the flag at Wimbledon as Marc Polmans and Storm Hunter make mixed final
items.summary: Marc Polmans and Storm Hunter win through to the Wimbledon mixed doubles final, as Thanasi Kokkinakis flies the flag in the men's doubles.

items.title: Shopping seasonally this July looks like ...
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Toddler waits seven hours at Bendigo Health amid pressure on emergency department
items.summary: A Central Victorian mother says she waited seven hours at Bendigo Health for her toddler to be treated for a head wound.

items.title: European politicians launch bid for investigation into FIFA boss Gianni Infantino red card controversy
items.summary: A group of European politicians launches a bid to begin an investigation into how and why a red card for an American football star was overturned at the World Cup.

items.title: Novak Djokovic wins five-hour Wimbledon epic over Felix Auger-Aliassime to set up Jannik Sinner semi
items.summary: Tennis legend Novak Djokovic sets up a Wimbledon semifinal blockbuster against Jannik Sinner after a five-hour epic.

items.title: Lancelin residents try to self-fund sea wall to stop imminent erosion
items.summary: Lancelin residents have been left to fundraise $150,000 for a sea wall in a bid to fight coastal erosion now seriously threatening the town, more than 100 kilometres north of Perth.

items.title: Great Barrier Reef's long-term monitoring program celebrates 40 years
items.summary: One of the researchers, who is often towed underwater to count fish and coral on the Great Barrier Reef, says it feels like flying.

items.title: Telstra outage brings interstate trains, Victorian regional rail network to a halt
items.summary: A crippling statewide cancellation of Victoria's regional trains, linked to a Telstra outage, is set to continue disrupting services into Thursday morning.

items.title: World Cup: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan fumes at ref after Argentina loss
items.summary: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan says his side "suffered an injustice" in defeat to Argentina, raging at the referee for a number of perceived errors.

items.title: Naomi Osaka says she's getting old after Wimbledon quarterfinal loss to Karolina Muchova
items.summary: Japan's Naomi Osaka bemoans getting old as her inspiring Wimbledon run comes to an end in the quarterfinals.

items.title: Iran targets sites in Bahrain and Kuwait after wave of US strikes
items.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards target US military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait after the US launched a wave of military strikes on Iran.

items.title: Seventeen dead, hundreds injured as China braces for more wild weather
items.summary: China's president calls for "all out" rescue efforts after storms kill at least 17 people and force the evacuation of tens of thousands.

items.title: Benjamin Netanyahu goes on US TV to urge Donald Trump not to sell jets to Türkiye
items.summary: The Israeli prime minister has appeared on American TV to urge the US president not to sell jet fighters to Türkiye, adding to a growing list of tensions between the leaders.

items.title: Telstra outage: 'Largely returned to business as usual' after nationwide crash
items.summary: Communications Minister Anika Wells says Telstra has "largely returned to business as usual" after its network suffered a nationwide outage that impacted potentially millions of Australians.

items.title: CFA senior leaders accidentally send 'draft' response to fatal bushfire inquiry
items.summary: An administrative error during a parliamentary inquiry has led the CFA to inadvertently reveal exactly how short-staffed its stations were during last summer's deadly fire season.

items.title: First-of-its-kind government report says AI not yet causing mass job lay-offs
items.summary: Artificial intelligence is not yet causing a broad disruption to Australia's labour market, according to a government report.

items.title: Algae which caused brevetoxins in SA algal bloom most toxic species studied of its kind
items.summary: The dominant species in the algal bloom which devastated SA's coastline, Karenia cristata, is the most toxic of its kind studied, according to newly published research.

items.title: Outback becomes oasis of birds, wildlife after 'unbelievable' desert rains
items.summary: Winged explorers of all colours, shapes and sizes flock to the desert's edge to breed in the bustling wetlands of the Channel Country.

items.title: Oral care giant Hismile used 'misleading' videos in social media advertisements
items.summary: Oral care giant Hismile has used "misleading" videos in social media advertisements showing a sales professional, and its own staff, spruiking products while dressed as health workers.

items.title: China missile launch tests Pacific relations as Australia looks to pounce
items.summary: This week, perhaps for the first time in a long time, some sections of the Pacific are signalling a slight adjustment to their relationship with China.

items.title: Country footy player finds new sport after suffering eight concussions
items.summary: Angus Pitman finds a new sporting passion after walking away from country footy due to coping eight concussions on the field.

items.title: Teammates of Melbourne footballer Nathan Fitzgerald to sit out match after death
items.summary: Epping football club's reserves players have decided to skip this weekend's game following the death of their beloved teammate Nathan Fitzgerald on Monday night.

items.title: What time is State of Origin III kick-off? How to watch the Maroons vs Blues decider
items.summary: The men's State of Origin series will be decided tonight, as Queensland hosts New South Wales at Lang Park in a winner-takes-all Game III. Here is what you need to know ahead of kick-off.

items.title: Video shows men who attended Khamenei's funeral and the son who didn't
items.summary: The strictly choreographed funeral of Iran's former supreme leader, ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has presented a rare insight into the image Tehran wants to project to the world and the men it sees as central to it.

items.title: How this school cracked the code to close the gap in early reading
items.summary: An inspiring turnaround at an Aboriginal community school could provide a scalable solution for closing the gap on Indigenous literacy.

items.title: PBS dispute could leave MS patients with huge bills for crucial drugs Ocrevus and Kesimpta
items.summary: The latest PBS stoush with drugmakers means thousands of people with MS are at risk of being forced to pay $33,000 a year for lifesaving medication.

items.title: Deadly podcasts to listen to this NAIDOC Week, from Indigenuity to Inside the Huddle
items.summary: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

items.title: Travel quiz, July 8: Jump in, we're going on a trip around the world
items.summary: Which Mediterranean island was a setting for The Godfather? And what's the capital city of Morocco?

items.title: Donald Trump lashes out at NATO leaders, while $72bn in arms deals struck at summit
items.summary: In a sign they are heeding calls from the United States to spend more to defend Europe, NATO leaders agree to deals worth about $72 billion.

items.title: Messi leads incredible Argentina comeback before Swiss shootout delight
items.summary: Lionel Messi guides Argentina to an incredible comeback win against Egypt, coming back from 2-0 down to steal a 3-2 win at the death, while Switzerland knocks Colombia out in a penalty shootout.

items.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage quits as an MP amid donation probe and vows to win by-election
items.summary: Nigel Farage, the far-right political leader riding a surge of support in Britain, has announced a major gamble that could end his political career.

items.title: Prince Harry, Elton John and others lose privacy lawsuits against the Daily Mail
items.summary: Prince Harry and six other claimants, including singer Elton John, have lost their claims against Daily Mail's publisher after alleging their phones were tapped, voicemails intercepted and personal information obtained through deception.

items.title: Marine Le Pen to enter French presidential race as court shortens ban on holding office
items.summary: The court in Paris upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for misusing EU funds but shortened her ban on running for elected office.

items.title: Ukrainian prosecutors confirm Monaco bomb suspect found dead
items.summary: A Ukrainian woman suspected of a bomb attack that targeted a wealthy Ukrainian-born businessman in Monaco has been found shot dead in Ukraine, according to Ukraine's prosecutor general's office.

items.title: Moment of explosion in Damascus during Macron visit
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Explosions rock Damascus near hotel hosting French president Emmanuel Macron
items.summary: French officials say Emmanuel Macron did not hear the blasts, and his motorcade continued through the streets of the Syrian capital to a meeting with President Ahmed Al-Sharaa, in the first visit by a European head of state since 2024.

items.title: Behind the scenes of Trump’s untethered second term as president
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: The world reacts to China’s missile test in the Pacific
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Toddler found in swimming pool and declared dead later found alive at hospital
items.summary: Five hours after the young boy was pronounced dead next to a suburban swimming pool in the US state of Arizona, police were notified that the child was breathing again at a local hospital.

items.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Former chancellor Michael Still defends UOW-KordaMentha deal at ICAC hearing
items.summary: An incoming vice-chancellor was providing input into a university review before his consulting firm later secured millions of dollars of related work, the ICAC has heard.

items.title: Piggery owners face court on animal cruelty charges
items.summary: Five co-owners of an Adelaide Plains piggery are collectively accused of ill-treating their animals, including allegedly failing to ensure the animals had "adequate and appropriate living conditions".

items.title: Report suggesting DPP Sally Dowling be removed over media leak condemned by attorney-general
items.summary: The inquiry into children's identity protection was called in October last year, but has focused almost solely on the leaking of the story to media.

items.title: Football club will hold service to honour Nathan Fitzgerald following fatal game day injury
items.summary: The younger brother of footy player and teacher Nathan Fitzgerald, who died last night, has described him as a "gentle, wonderful" soul who cherished his family.

items.title: Police officer punched man at Cabramatta station
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Officer filmed punching man during arrest was using an 'approved manoeuvre', NSW Police say
items.summary: NSW Police say an officer filmed repeatedly punching a man in the back of the head on in Western Sydney on Monday night was using an "approved manoeuvre".

items.title: Suspected bird flu identified in giant petrel on SA coast, but testing to determine if H5
items.summary: A suspected H5 bird flu case has been detected in a giant petrel on SA's Yorke Peninsula, but the state government says the viral load is low, with further results expected within the next 48 hours.

items.title: Launceston General Hospital emergency department overflowing, nurses union says
items.summary: Launceston General Hospital's emergency department was overflowing on Monday night, with the nursing union describing the scenes as "chaotic" and "unsafe" — as Tasmania's health minister blames the federal government for a lack of action.

items.title: Accused double killer Beau Lamarre-Condon's mother Coleen Lamarre granted bail
items.summary: Coleen Lamarre has been in custody since April, when she was arrested in Sydney's inner west accused of attempting to influence a key witness in her son's upcoming double murder trial.

items.title: Report shows almost half of Qld prisoners returning to custody within two years
items.summary: The auditor-general has found Queensland Corrective Services (QCS) is not effectively planning or facilitating the rehabilitation and reintegration of its prisoners.

items.title: World Cup: Belgium consoles Folarin Balogun but claims 'justice' was served in 4-1 win over US
items.summary: Belgium did not feel it was fair Folarin Balogun played for the US in the round of 16 match after he was given a red card in his team's previous match.

items.title: Widow fights for answers over death of Laurence Harbeck on INPEX LNG plant
items.summary: A Perth widow says she has been kept in the dark about the circumstances leading to the death of her husband as he worked on an offshore gas plant.

items.title: Fishing ban lifted early in SA amid positive signs for King George whiting
items.summary: A temporary ban on recreational catches of King George whiting along parts of the SA coast is lifted earlier than scheduled, in a move that fishers say will benefit the whole sector.

items.title: World Cup quick hits: American dream comes crashing down, Kylian Mbappé hits back at abuse
items.summary: The US goes crashing out of its own World Cup after days of controversy and rancour, while Kylian Mbappé refuses to stand for dreadful racist abuse. Here are today's quick hits.

items.title: Government commits to restore current Paradise Dam wall over new build
items.summary: The Queensland government announces a plan to rebuild the Paradise Dam wall where it is, despite its operator previously saying there were too many issues to fix it.

items.title: Adelaide teen 'fight club' condemned by SA premier and police minister
items.summary: South Australia's premier says he is appalled by an amateur children's "fight club", while the state's police minister says people who post videos of the fights online may face legal action on top of the participants in the bouts.

items.title: With one missile, China sends world a reminder of its growing military power
items.summary: This latest military test demonstrates China's growing willingness to use its military strength to send a warning to the Pacific: that Beijing now has the military and nuclear means to assert its dominance in the region.

items.title: NRL announces new broadcast rights deal worth estimated $5.3 billion starting after 2027 season
items.summary: As the NRL reveals its new broadcast rights deal worth billions of dollars over seven years in 2028 through 2034, executives try to hose down concerns about rising subscription prices.

items.title: Why State of Origin legacies and futures are on the line for New South Wales in Game III
items.summary: As New South Wales look to rise from the ashes and pull off a Lang Park Origin heist, the future of the team and the legacy of some of its greatest players will be tested in the furnace of a decider.

items.title: AFL legend Tony Modra leaves hospital after truck crash near Victor Harbor
items.summary: Former Adelaide Crows and Fremantle player Tony Modra says he has left hospital after he was seriously injured in a truck crash last month.

items.title: ADFA cadet facing 79 additional charges after allegedly using hidden bathroom camera to film women
items.summary: Police allege Tain Stephens was living at the Australian Defence Force Academy in Canberra when he hid a camera inside a soap dispenser in a female bathroom to capture intimate recordings of women.

items.title: Man denied bail after drugs allegedly seized from vehicle in prison car park
items.summary: Wayne Douglas Carnell has been remanded in custody after police allegedly found 100 grams of methamphetamine in his vehicle which was searched in a prison car park.

items.title: Former NRL player Jai Arrow marries partner months after motor neurone disease diagnosis
items.summary: The former Rabbitohs and Queensland NRL player has married his partner Berina Colakovic in a small ceremony in Sydney, months after revealing his motor neurone disease diagnosis.

items.title: New steel plant for Newcastle nearly three decades on from BHP's closure
items.summary: Once dubbed Australia's "steel city", steel production wound down in Newcastle in 1999 when BHP closed its plant. Now, an Australian company says it will build a new steel plant, powered only by electricity.
